title: Matelea diversifolia
taxonomy: Matelea diversifolia is classified within the Apocynaceae family, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This family of plants consists of over 5,000 species, including a wide range of succulents, shrubs, and trees. As a member of the Apocynaceae family, Matelea diversifolia is further classified within the subfamily Asclepioideae, which includes over 2,000 species of plants. The subfamily Asclepioideae is characterized by its unique flowers and seed pods. Matelea diversifolia's classification provides valuable information for botanists and researchers studying the plant's characteristics and evolutionary history.

intro: Matelea diversifolia, also known as "wild cancel," is a tropical vine plant species native to the Americas, characterized by its unique, twisted stems and small white flowers. Traditional medicinal uses of Matelea diversifolia have been documented, with reported health benefits including antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate conditions such as muscle spasms, pain, and oxidative stress. Bioactive constituents of Matelea diversifolia include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
